title: core.experiment.ExperimentControl
---



```python
core.experiment.ExperimentControl(
    dataset,
    model_class,
    hyperparameters,
    seed=123,
    device='cpu',
    num_workers=0,
    epochs=None,
    batch_size=32,
    optimizer_class=None,

```

Controls the experiment configuration.

This data class serves as the central configuration object for optimization experiments,
holding the dataset, model configuration, hyperparameters, and other settings.

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

| Name            | Type                                                 | Description                                            |
|-----------------|------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| dataset         | [SpotDataSet](`spotoptim.core.data.SpotDataSet`)     | The dataset object containing the data.                |
| model_class     | [Any](`typing.Any`)                                  | The class of the model to be instantiated.             |
| hyperparameters | [Any](`typing.Any`)                                  | The hyperparameters for the model.                     |
| seed            | [int](`int`)                                         | The random seed for reproducibility.                   |
| device          | [str](`str`)                                         | The device to run the model on (e.g. "cpu" or "cuda"). |
| num_workers     | [int](`int`)                                         | The number of workers to use for data loading.         |
| epochs          | [Optional](`typing.Optional`)\[[int](`int`)\]        | The number of epochs to train the model.               |
| batch_size      | [int](`int`)                                         | The batch size for training.                           |
| optimizer_class | [Optional](`typing.Optional`)\[[Any](`typing.Any`)\] | The optimizer class to use for training.               |
| loss_function   | [Optional](`typing.Optional`)\[[Any](`typing.Any`)\] | The loss function to use for training.                 |
| metrics         | [List](`typing.List`)\[[str](`str`)\]                | The metrics to track during training.                  |
| n_initial       | [int](`int`)                                         | The number of initial design points.                   |
| max_evals       | [int](`int`)                                         | The maximum number of evaluations.                     |
| experiment_name | [str](`str`)                                         | The name of the experiment.                            |
| verbosity       | [int](`int`)                                         | The verbosity level.                                   |

## Public Methods {.doc-section .doc-section-public-methods}

to_dict(): Convert the object to a dictionary.
torch_device(): Return the torch device object.

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}


::: {#0c15bd54 .cell execution_count=1}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import numpy as np
import torch
from spotoptim.core.data import SpotDataFromArray
from spotoptim.core.experiment import ExperimentControl
from spotoptim.nn.mlp import MLP

# 1. Prepare Data
X = np.array([[0.1, 0.2], [0.3, 0.4]])
y = np.array([[1.0], [2.0]])
dataset = SpotDataFromArray(x_train=X, y_train=y)

# 2. Define Hyperparameters
params = {"l1": 16, "num_hidden_layers": 1, "lr": 1e-3}

# 3. Initialize Control with Real Model
exp = ExperimentControl(
    dataset=dataset,
    model_class=MLP,
    hyperparameters=params,
    experiment_name="real_model_run",
    seed=42
)
print(exp.to_dict())
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
{'dataset': <spotoptim.core.data.SpotDataFromArray object at 0x11fc4c050>, 'model_class': <class 'spotoptim.nn.mlp.MLP'>, 'hyperparameters': {'l1': 16, 'num_hidden_layers': 1, 'lr': 0.001}, 'seed': 42, 'device': 'cpu', 'num_workers': 0, 'epochs': None, 'batch_size': 32, 'optimizer_class': None, 'loss_function': None, 'metrics': [], 'n_initial': 10, 'max_evals': 50, 'experiment_name': 'real_model_run', 'verbosity': 0}
```

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

::: {#e02fe7ec .cell execution_count=2}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import numpy as np
from spotoptim.core.data import SpotDataFromArray
from spotoptim.core.experiment import ExperimentControl

# Setup
dataset = SpotDataFromArray(np.zeros((5,2)), np.zeros((5,1)))
exp = ExperimentControl(dataset, model_class=None, hyperparameters={})

# Convert to dict
config = exp.to_dict()
print(config['seed'])
```
